metadata.defaultTitle: Esha Sharma — Master Wordsmith & Brand Voice Architect
metadata.description: Esha Sharma is a content writer and copywriter in Melbourne & Kathmandu. Brand voice, conversion copy and long-form narrative that sounds like a person, not a template.
metadata.ogTitle: Esha Sharma — Master Wordsmith & Brand Voice Architect
metadata.ogDescription: Brand voice, conversion copy and long-form narrative that sounds like a person, not a template.

hero.title: Esha Sharma
hero.tagline: I turn quiet brands into ones people quote out loud.
hero.subtitle: A Melbourne & Kathmandu copywriter writing brand voice, long-form narrative and conversion copy that sounds like a person — not a template.

about.headline: A writer who treats language like architecture.
bio: I am Esha Sharma, a content writer and copywriter working between Melbourne and Kathmandu. For nine years I have helped founders, editors and brand teams find the single sentence that does the heavy lifting.

My work lives at the seam of strategy and story: voice guidelines that survive contact with a real marketing calendar, landing pages that convert without shouting, and long-form pieces people actually finish reading at 11pm.
about.philosophy: Good copy is not decoration. It is the difference between a brand people scroll past and a brand people remember. I write the second kind — and I treat every word as if a real human is waiting on the other side of the screen.

journey.title: First byline
journey.detail: Staff writer at a Kathmandu lifestyle magazine. Learned to file on deadline and kill my darlings.

journey.title: Agency years
journey.detail: Senior copywriter in Melbourne. Shipped voice for fintech, hospitality and a very opinionated coffee brand.

journey.title: Independent
journey.detail: Founded a studio of one, partnering with brands who care about the words as much as the logo.

journey.title: Voice architect
journey.detail: Now embedded as brand voice lead for scale-ups across ANZ and South Asia.

services.title: Brand Voice
services.desc: A voice system your whole team can actually use — principles, a do/don't library and real before-and-after rewrites.

services.title: Conversion Copy
services.desc: Landing pages and funnels written to move people, not just impress them. Strategy first, words second.

services.title: Long-form
services.desc: Essays, articles and brand stories that earn attention and hold it. The kind people screenshot.

portfolio.title: Giving a fintech a human pulse
portfolio.challenge: Lumen's app was clever but cold. Support tickets showed users trusted the maths, not the brand. Every email read like a terms-of-service page.
portfolio.strategy: We built a warm, plain-spoken voice and rewrote every onboarding moment around one promise: money without the mystery. No jargon, no fake enthusiasm — just a calm human explaining the thing.
portfolio.testimonial: Esha gave us a voice we could finally stand behind. Our users noticed within a week.

portfolio.title: A menu that makes you hungry
portfolio.challenge: A beautiful restaurant with a website that read like a spreadsheet. Bookings were flat and the booking flow felt like a tax form.
portfolio.strategy: Rewrote the menu and booking flow as a small story — sensory, specific, a little cheeky. Led with the smell of the kitchen, not the opening hours.
portfolio.testimonial: Our tables filled up before we changed a single recipe. The words did the work.

portfolio.title: A journal worth the climb
portfolio.challenge: A travel brand with stunning photos and forgettable words. Readers bounced before the second paragraph.
portfolio.strategy: A six-part editorial series written like letters home, not brochures. Specific weather, specific people, specific silences.
portfolio.testimonial: People emailed us to say they cried. That is the point of the whole thing.
portfolio.testimonial_author: Anjali Sherpa, Marketing Lead, Himalaya Trails

blog.title: Why your brand voice dies in the second sprint
blog.excerpt: A voice document is not a spell. It is a habit. Here is how to make it stick past launch week.
body: Every brand starts with a voice doc. Most of them are dead within two months, and nobody is surprised.
The reason is simple: the document is treated as a deliverable, not a discipline. The wordsmith leaves, the new hire guesses, and the tone drifts back to safe and forgettable.
Make the voice a checklist in the pull request, not a PDF on a shelf. If a sentence can't survive review, it doesn't ship.

blog.title: The one sentence that does the selling
blog.excerpt: You do not need more copy. You need one sentence that earns the rest of the page.
body: Most landing pages fail in the first line. Not the headline — the promise hidden underneath it.
Find the sentence a reader would say to a friend. Then build the entire page around it and delete everything that competes with it.

testimonials.quote: Esha writes the sentence the whole team wishes they had said out loud.
testimonials.author: Priya Nair

testimonials.quote: Our bookings doubled and the words finally felt like us, not like an agency.
testimonials.author: Tom Reilly

testimonials.quote: She made a trail journal people actually finished reading. Miracle.
testimonials.author: Anjali Sherpa

services.title: Considered words, priced like the investment they are.

contact.title: Tell me what you're building. I'll tell you what it should say.
contact.freeOfferDesc: Send a note and I'll review three pages of your current copy — free, no strings — and tell you the one sentence worth changing first.

process.d: We map the brand, the audience and the gap between them.
I draft voice principles and the first real sentences.
Tight edits until every word earns its place.
You get copy your whole team can use with confidence.

faq.a: Book a free Brand Voice Audit. I'll read your current words and tell you what's working and what's quietly costing you trust.
Yes — often as the embedded wordsmith behind an agency's brand work, under NDA.
Most projects land in 1–3 weeks. Voice systems take longer; single landing pages, faster.

beats.title: The Idea
beats.copy: Every project begins as a single, stubborn thought. We find the one worth keeping.

beats.title: The Words
beats.copy: Then we shape it — voice, rhythm, restraint. Sentences that carry weight without raising their voice.

beats.title: The Impact
beats.copy: And it lands. Readers lean in, remember, and repeat it to someone they trust.

footer.headline: Let's write something worth reading
footer.tagline: Made between two cities, with care.
